title: pseudognaphalium undulatum
intro: Pseudognaphalium undulatum, also known as coast ragwort, is a shrub-like flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Western Australia, characterized by its silver-gray leaves and bright yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium undulatum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a topical ointment, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
